19: This code demonstrates a simple application using the Crew.ai library to simulate a
20: financial analysis task. It defines a custom tool, get_stock_price, that simulates looking
21: up stock prices for predefined tickers. The tool is designed to return a floating-point
22: number for valid tickers or raise a ValueError for invalid ones. A Crew.ai Agent named
23: financial_analyst_agent is created with the role of a Senior Financial Analyst. This
24: agent is given the get_stock_price tool to interact with. A Task is defined,
25: analyze_aapl_task, specifically instructing the agent to find the simulated stock price
26: for AAPL using the tool. The task description includes clear instructions on how to
27: handle both success and failure cases when using the tool. A Crew is assembled,
28: comprising the financial_analyst_agent and the analyze_aapl_task. The verbose
29: setting is enabled for both the agent and the crew to provide detailed logging during
30: execution. The main part of the script runs the crew's task using the kickoff() method
31: within a standard if __name__ == "__main__": block. Before starting the crew, it checks
32: if the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable is set, which is required for the agent to
33: function. The result of the crew's execution, which is the output of the task, is then
34: printed to the console. The code also includes basic logging configuration for better
35: tracking of the crew's actions and tool calls. It uses environment variables for API key
